questions.q: By what affectionate epithet was the legendary playback singer Lata Mangeshkar widely known?
options: The Melody Queen of South India
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar was popularly called the 'Nightingale of India' for her clear, enduring voice across Indian cinema.

questions.q: In which year was the playback singer Lata Mangeshkar born?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar was born on 28 September 1929 in Indore.

questions.q: In which year did the singer Lata Mangeshkar pass away?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar died on 6 February 2022 in Mumbai at the age of 92.

questions.q: India's highest civilian honour, the Bharat Ratna, was conferred on Lata Mangeshkar in which year?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar received the Bharat Ratna in 2001, only the second vocalist (after M. S. Subbulakshmi) so honoured.

questions.q: Lata Mangeshkar received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, India's highest film honour, in which year?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar was honoured with the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 1989 for her contribution to Indian cinema.

questions.q: Lata Mangeshkar's celebrated younger sister, also a famous playback singer, is:
questions.explanation: Asha Bhosle, Lata Mangeshkar's younger sister, was herself a renowned and prolific playback singer.

questions.q: In which Indian city was the singer Lata Mangeshkar born?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar was born in Indore, then part of the Indore State in central India.

questions.q: Lata Mangeshkar primarily rose to fame as which kind of artist in Indian cinema?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar was a playback singer who recorded songs that actresses lip-synced on screen.

questions.q: The patriotic song 'Aye Mere Watan Ke Logon', famously sung by Lata Mangeshkar, moved which leader to tears when she performed it in 1963?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar's 1963 rendition of 'Aye Mere Watan Ke Logon' reportedly brought Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru to tears.

questions.q: Lata Mangeshkar recorded songs in over how many languages during her long career?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar recorded in over thirty languages across Hindi and regional Indian cinema.

questions.q: What was the surname Lata Mangeshkar's family adopted, derived from their ancestral town of Mangeshi in Goa?
questions.explanation: The family adopted the surname Mangeshkar after their native town Mangeshi in Goa, replacing Hardikar.

questions.q: Lata Mangeshkar's father, Deenanath Mangeshkar, was renowned chiefly as a:
questions.explanation: Deenanath Mangeshkar was a noted classical vocalist and Marathi stage (sangeet natak) singer-actor.

questions.q: Which 1949 film's hit song 'Aayega Aanewala' is widely regarded as the breakthrough that established Lata Mangeshkar as a leading playback singer?
questions.explanation: 'Aayega Aanewala' from the 1949 film Mahal, picturised on Madhubala, brought Lata Mangeshkar nationwide fame.

questions.q: With which music composer did Lata Mangeshkar record the influential early hit 'Aayega Aanewala' and the rest of the score of Mahal (1949)?
questions.explanation: Khemchand Prakash composed 'Aayega Aanewala' for Mahal, an early career landmark for Lata Mangeshkar.

questions.q: For which song did Lata Mangeshkar win her first Filmfare Award for Best Female Playback Singer, from the 1958 film Madhumati?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar won her first Filmfare Best Female Playback Singer award for 'Aaja Re Pardesi' from Madhumati (1958).

questions.q: Which composer of Madhumati (1958), for which Lata Mangeshkar won her first Filmfare playback award, was known for songs like 'Suhana Safar'?
questions.explanation: Salil Chowdhury composed the music for Madhumati, including 'Aaja Re Pardesi' sung by Lata Mangeshkar.

questions.q: Lata Mangeshkar had a famously close musical partnership with which composer, known for ghazal-tinged melodies such as 'Lag Ja Gale' and 'Naina Barse'?
questions.explanation: Madan Mohan and Lata Mangeshkar shared a celebrated partnership, producing classics like 'Lag Ja Gale' and 'Naina Barse' (both from Woh Kaun Thi, 1964).

questions.q: In which 1960 historical epic did Lata Mangeshkar sing 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya', picturised on Madhubala?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar sang 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya' in the 1960 epic Mughal-e-Azam, composed by Naushad.

questions.q: Which male playback singer formed one of Hindi cinema's most famous duet pairings with Lata Mangeshkar in Raj Kapoor's films, on songs like 'Sawan Ka Mahina'?
questions.explanation: Mukesh and Lata Mangeshkar were a beloved duet pair; 'Sawan Ka Mahina' from Milan (1967) is among their well-known duets.

questions.q: Lata Mangeshkar famously never recorded a single song with which composer, who instead favoured Asha Bhosle and Geeta Dutt?
questions.explanation: O. P. Nayyar is well known for never recording a song with Lata Mangeshkar, relying on Asha Bhosle and Geeta Dutt instead.

questions.q: Lata Mangeshkar sang the haunting songs of which 1972 Kamal Amrohi film starring Meena Kumari, including 'Chalte Chalte' and 'Inhi Logon Ne'?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar lent her voice to the songs of Pakeezah (1972), one of the most cherished soundtracks in Hindi cinema.

questions.q: Under which pseudonym did Lata Mangeshkar occasionally compose music for Marathi films?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar composed music for several Marathi films under the pseudonym Anandghan.

questions.q: Lata Mangeshkar's only brother, who became a noted music composer working with her on Marathi and Hindi songs, was named:
questions.explanation: Hridaynath Mangeshkar, the youngest sibling, was a composer who collaborated with Lata Mangeshkar on works including Marathi abhangs.

questions.q: Roughly how old was Lata Mangeshkar when her father died, prompting her to start working to support the family in the early 1940s?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar was about 13 when her father Deenanath died in 1942, leading her to begin singing and acting for income.

questions.q: Which music director was an early champion of Lata Mangeshkar in the late 1940s, giving her a key break with the song 'Dil Mera Toda' in the film Majboor (1948)?
questions.explanation: Composer Ghulam Haider championed Lata Mangeshkar early on, giving her the break 'Dil Mera Toda' in Majboor (1948) when others doubted her voice.

questions.q: Lata Mangeshkar's 1963 performance of 'Aye Mere Watan Ke Logon' commemorated Indian soldiers who died in which conflict?
questions.explanation: The song honoured soldiers killed in the 1962 Sino-Indian War; Lata Mangeshkar first performed it in early 1963.

questions.q: Who composed 'Aye Mere Watan Ke Logon', the patriotic song made immortal by Lata Mangeshkar?
questions.explanation: C. Ramchandra composed 'Aye Mere Watan Ke Logon', with lyrics by Kavi Pradeep, sung by Lata Mangeshkar.

questions.q: Lata Mangeshkar appeared as a child actress and singer in films before becoming a full-time playback singer; in which decade did this early acting work occur?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar acted in and sang for several Marathi and Hindi films during the 1940s before focusing on playback.

questions.q: Which composer duo, prolific from the 1960s onward, became one of Lata Mangeshkar's most frequent collaborators on hits like the songs of Bobby and many others?
questions.explanation: Laxmikant-Pyarelal were among Lata Mangeshkar's most frequent and long-running composing collaborators, including the music of Bobby (1973).

questions.q: In her later career, Lata Mangeshkar recorded for Yash Chopra's 2004 film Veer-Zaara, whose songs were built from unused tunes left behind by which late composer?
questions.explanation: Veer-Zaara (2004) used unused melodies of the late Madan Mohan, arranged by his son Sanjeev Kohli, with Lata Mangeshkar singing several songs.
